page: Amazon Web Services m5d.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8175M processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU supports hardware-level features such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES cryptography, and BMI2, though it lacks newer instruction sets like AVX-512 VNNI or AMX.

This instance includes 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high-speed local instance store volumes directly attached to the host. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The inclusion of local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed temporary storage, eliminating the need for external block storage for scratch space.

Benchmark data indicates that the m5d.8xlarge exhibits weak single-core performance, as seen in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. However, its multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations score in the average range. The instance is suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, Redis databases, and medium-sized LLM inference tasks, while large-scale LLM inference is constrained by CPU performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8175M processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. While single-core benchmark results are weak, the instance delivers average multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. This hardware profile makes it a balanced choice for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database workloads that benefit from high-speed local storage without requiring specialized GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1200 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR4 memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM
1200 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD instance storage
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and Redis databases
